success_surface: Precise cell-range copying using openpyxl iteration with validation
effective_behaviors: Used openpyxl for targeted cell value replacement within specified ranges
Scoped operations using answer_position metadata rather than worksheet dimensions
Implemented defensive null checks before overwriting cell values

memory_items.title: OpenPyXL Targeted Cell Replacement
memory_items.lesson: For precise cell value updates within defined ranges, use openpyxl cell iteration instead of pandas bulk operations.
memory_items.generalization: When modifying specific cell ranges identified by position metadata, prefer openpyxl's iterative cell access over pandas dataframe operations to preserve workbook structure and avoid unintended formatting loss.

memory_items.title: Answer Position Range Scoping
memory_items.lesson: Use answer_position or instruction-specified ranges directly rather than inferring from worksheet dimensions.
memory_items.generalization: Always anchor modification loops to explicitly specified cell ranges or positions from task metadata instead of deriving scope from ws.max_row/column which may include irrelevant data.

memory_items.title: Defensive Value Assignment
memory_items.lesson: Check for None/null values before overwriting cells to prevent accidental blank cell creation.
memory_items.generalization: Implement conditional checks (if source_value is not None) before assigning cell values to ensure valid data propagation and handle missing source data gracefully.

memory_items.title: Operation Count Validation
memory_items.lesson: Log the number of successfully modified cells to enable quick sanity checks.
memory_items.generalization: After executing batch modifications, print or record the actual count of changed cells against the expected range size to verify complete execution.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Targeted Cell Operations
- **Range Scoping:** When task metadata specifies exact cell positions or ranges, use those bounds directly instead of relying on worksheet dimension methods like max_row or max_column.
- **Tool Selection:** For precise cell-value replacements across defined ranges, use openpyxl's iterative cell access rather than pandas dataframe operations to preserve existing workbook structure and minimize side effects.
- **Value Safety:** Always validate source values are not None before overwriting destination cells to prevent unintentional blank cells.
- **Execution Verification:** Track and log the count of successfully modified cells immediately after batch operations to enable quick validation against expected deltas.
